commit.message:
[gpu] Fix contour breaks and bounds quantization in encode (#5892)

Encode used adjacent endpoint equality to infer contour breaks, which
fails when two contours touch at the same point. Track contour starts
explicitly during accumulation instead, and use that flag when packing
shared endpoints.

Encode also rounded blob bounds to the nearest quarter unit. That can
shrink the encoded extents and clip coverage at glyph edges. Quantize
mins downward and maxes upward so the blob header always encloses the
actual geometry.

Add focused API tests for both regressions.

Testing:
- meson test -C build

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive NDJSON raw logging layer for offline L0/L1/L2 analysis

Every NDJSON event now carries run_id and schema_version. The log is
bounded by run_started / run_finished (terminal marker) so an offline
job can detect incomplete runs. API calls are split into
api_call_started / api_call_finished with stable call_id, usage tokens,
finish_reason, and raw response data. Normalizers return _normMeta
describing which extraction path was taken (json, keyword fallback,
default, truncation). Utterance cleaning reports per-step booleans
(speaker prefix, actions, hallucination, min-length, dedup). Collision
resolution emits per-round collision_round events across all four tiers.
Interruption evaluation emits a single interruption_evaluation event
with all listeners, representative selection, and resolution method.

No changes to discussion semantics, prompt wording, or resolution logic.

commit.message:
Add CLI entry point with comprehensive NDJSON logging, switch to pnpm

New CLI (src/cli.ts) reads a JSON config file and runs a full roundtable
discussion with colorful terminal output and a detailed NDJSON log file
capturing all API requests/responses (including reasoning tokens), events,
thoughts, and session state. Adds ApiCallHook to the OpenAI provider for
instrumentation (best-effort, cannot affect request semantics). Switches
package manager from npm to pnpm and adds tsx as a dev dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: sync sidebar terminal background with VS Code theme (#565)

* fix(webview): sync terminal theme background

Summary:
- centralize VS Code terminal theme resolution so webview and xterm
  prefer terminal.background over editor.background
- update UI theme refresh to repaint root terminal containers when the
  active VS Code theme changes
- add regression tests for theme resolution and background sync

Rationale:
- built-in terminals and the sidebar terminal diverged when a theme or
  workbench color customization set terminal.background
- a shared resolver reduces future regressions from CSS and xterm using
  different background fallbacks

Tests:
- npm run compile
- npm run test:unit
- npm run lint (fails on pre-existing repo-wide lint errors; changed
  files are error-free under targeted eslint)

commit.message:
Harden config validation, add requestStop API, fix multi-line blockquotes and observer threading

- Separate config normalization (mutating, private) from validation (pure, exported)
  to prevent mutating caller-owned data when createSession calls validateConfig
- Add Number.isFinite/isInteger checks for numeric config fields
- Add requestStop(session) for graceful manual stop (stopRequested flag)
- Thread SessionObserver through collision/interruption for onThoughtUpdate callbacks
- Fix multi-line utterances in history projection (bq helper) and turn directives
- Add thinkingModel flag: provider adapter applies 10x max_tokens for reasoning models
- Truncate verbose/HTML error bodies in OpenAI provider
- Fix voting candidate parsing for formatNameList output styles
- Add [Name]: prefix stripping and post-strip history hallucination re-check
- Fix @mention pattern with word boundary (?=\W|$)
- Restore original reaction insistence on collision winner for SpeechRecord
- Update docs (DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PROVIDER.md) to reflect all changes
